title: Amoria vesiculosa

The species Amoria vesiculosa is further classified within the phylum Mollusca and the class Bivalvia. The family Veneridae is a large group of bivalve mollusks that includes many species of clams and oysters. Amoria vesiculosa is native to the coastal waters of the Indo-Pacific region, where it inhabits shallow waters and sandy or muddy substrates.

intro:

However, there is a plant called Amorpha fruticosa, also known as Desert False Indigo or Amorpha. 

Amorpha fruticosa is a shrub in the legume family (Fabaceae) and is native to North America.

It has been reported to have potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
